data.title: Travel + Leisure posts 2025 results, boosts 2026 outlook
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Travel + Leisure Co ( (TNL) ) has provided an announcement.

Travel + Leisure Co. reported its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 results on February 18, 2026, posting 2025 net revenue of $4.02 billion and net income of $230 million, despite $216 million in inventory write-downs tied to a Resort Optimization Initiative. Vacation Ownership drove 8% growth in full-year Gross VOI sales to $2.49 billion and margin improvement, while the Travel and Membership segment saw revenue and Adjusted EBITDA declines amid lower revenue per transaction and a mix shift toward lower-margin travel club activity.

For the fourth quarter of 2025, the company generated net revenue of $1.03 billion but recorded a net loss of $61 million due to $210 million of impairments related to resort optimization, even as Adjusted EBITDA rose to $272 million. Management exceeded its 2025 outlook, strengthened liquidity to $1.15 billion, refinanced term debt at a lower interest rate, executed $300 million of share repurchases and $149 million of dividends for the year, and outlined 2026 guidance that calls for higher Adjusted EBITDA, continued VOI sales growth, and a positive financial impact from the Resort Optimization Initiative alongside a new $750 million share buyback authorization and a planned dividend increase.

The most recent analyst rating on (TNL) stock is a Buy with a $80.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Travel + Leisure Co stock, see the TNL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TNL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TNL is a Outperform.

Travel + Leisure Co’s overall stock score is driven by strong technical indicators and attractive valuation, supported by positive earnings call sentiment. However, the financial performance is mixed, with significant leverage concerns on the balance sheet. Recent corporate events positively impact the score by enhancing financial flexibility.

To see Spark’s full report on TNL stock, click here.

More about Travel + Leisure Co

Travel + Leisure Co., listed on the NYSE under the ticker TNL, is a leading leisure travel company focused on vacation ownership and related travel and membership services. Its core Vacation Ownership segment generates the majority of revenue through vacation ownership interest (VOI) sales, while its Travel and Membership business provides travel clubs and related services, with a focus on delivering recurring fee-based income and enhancing owner experiences across its resort portfolio.

Average Trading Volume: 570,546

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $4.56B

For an in-depth examination of TNL stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.title: Horizon Space Acquisition II Extends Timeline for SL Bio Deal
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Horizon Space Acquisition II Corp. ( (HSPT) ) just unveiled an update.

On February 13, 2026, Horizon Space Acquisition II Corp. shareholders approved amendments to its trust agreement and charter, allowing the SPAC to extend its deadline to complete an initial business combination from February 18, 2026, in up to twelve one-month increments to as late as February 18, 2027, while maintaining the framework to redeem all public shares if no deal is completed. The company subsequently executed the trust amendment on February 17, 2026, aligning the trust account liquidation mechanics with the new potential timetable and monthly deposit structure.

At a separate meeting on February 12, 2026, shareholders also approved the proposed business combination with SL BIO Ltd. via a two-step merger into a new Cayman holding company, SL Science Holding Limited, and confirmed related amendments to PubCo’s governing documents and the appointment of William Wang Ching-Dong as HSPT’s sole director at the merger effective time. The strong shareholder turnout and approvals secure both the transaction framework with SL Bio and additional time to close it, reducing deal-termination risk and clarifying the path forward for investors awaiting de-SPAC completion.

More about Horizon Space Acquisition II Corp.

Horizon Space Acquisition II Corp. is a Cayman Islands-based special purpose acquisition company, or SPAC, formed to consummate a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization or similar business combination. The company raised capital in an initial public offering completed on November 18, 2024, and holds investors’ funds in a trust account pending completion of a suitable business combination.

Average Trading Volume: 24,248

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $115.4M

For a thorough assessment of HSPT stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Fluor’s Federal Contract Dependence Poses Heightened Revenue, Regulatory and Reputational Risks
data.description:
Fluor Corporation (FLR) has disclosed a new risk, in the Sales & Marketing category.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fluor Corporation’s heavy reliance on U.S. federal contracts, including DOE and DOD work, exposes it to appropriations delays, shifting budget priorities, shutdowns and stop-work orders that can abruptly reduce revenue, disrupt project execution and impair cash flow. Layered regulatory regimes (FAR, CAS, Truth in Negotiations Act and related security rules), intensive audits and competitive rebid processes heighten the risk of pricing pressure, payment withholds, contract terminations, potential penalties or debarment, and reputational damage that could constrain future government and private-sector opportunities.

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on FLR stock based on 2 Buys and 2 Holds.

To learn more about Fluor Corporation’s risk factors, click here.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

Waste Management has seen notable insider activity, with two senior executives cashing out significant holdings. Sr. VP of Operations Donald J. Smith sold 12,194 shares of Waste Management stock in a transaction totaling $2,847,055, while SVP of Enterprise Strategy Rafael Carrasco sold 7,352 shares for proceeds of $1,724,044. These transactions highlight substantial profit-taking at the upper management level, drawing attention to how key leaders are managing their personal stakes in the company.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WM is a Outperform.
